brain.title: Memory
brain.subtitle: All durable facts the agents have learned

empty.title: No memories yet
empty.description: Connect an AI provider in Settings → Providers and let your agents start learning.

timeline.title: Memory timeline
timeline.subtitle: Chronological log of what your agents learned and when. Newest first.
timeline.empty: No memory events in this period yet.
timeline.error: Couldn't load the timeline. Try refreshing.

diff.title: Memory diff
diff.subtitle: What changed in memory across the selected window.
diff.emptyAdded: No new facts in this window.
diff.emptyForgotten: No facts forgotten in this window.
diff.emptyUpdated: No fact edits in this window.
diff.error: Couldn't load the diff. Try refreshing.

export.title: Export your memory
export.subtitle: All durable facts, decisions, relations, and citations the agents have learned about your workspace.
export.jsonDescription: Full export — facts, decisions, relations, citations. Best for reimport or analysis.
export.csvDescription: One row per fact. Doesn't include decisions or relations.
export.error: Couldn't generate export. Please try again.
export.notReady: Export isn't available yet — try again in a minute.

schema.facts: id, scope, kind, subject, statement, confidence, pinned, status, createdAt, updatedAt.
schema.decisions: Distilled decisions logged by agents (id, statement, rationale, links to source facts).
schema.relations: Edges between facts (subject ↔ predicate ↔ object, with confidence).
schema.citations: Pointers to the source messages that produced each fact — message IDs, not message bodies.

export.privacyBody: This export contains everything your agents remember about this workspace. It does not include source conversations themselves — only references to them (message IDs).

undo.title: Recent changes
undo.subtitle: The last 20 changes to memory. Revert any of them from the last 7 days.
undo.windowNote: Only changes from the last 7 days are revertible. Older entries are shown for reference.
undo.comingSoonBody: We'll show every memory change here as soon as the audit feed lands.
undo.error: Couldn't load the change log. Try refreshing.
undo.revertUnsupported: This change can't be reverted automatically yet.

sensitivity.toggleHelp: Pause memory extraction for this conversation. You can turn it back on anytime.
sensitivity.bannerPaused: Memory learning is paused for this conversation.

timeTravel.label: View memory as of:
timeTravel.banner: Viewing historical state from {date}. Edits are disabled in time-travel mode.
timeTravel.editsDisabled: Edits are disabled in time-travel mode

review.title: Review queue
review.subtitle: Facts the agents flagged as low-confidence or contradictory. Resolve them so the recall pool stays trustworthy.
review.emptyDescription: Nothing needs your attention right now. New items show up when the workers flag a fact as low-confidence or contradictory.

heartbeat.title: Your AI is learning on its own

step1.title: Your agents talk
step1.body: Every conversation your agents have — with customers, with your team, or with each other — feeds Orchester.

step2.title: Memory learns automatically
step2.body: When a conversation ends, Orchester extracts the durable facts: preferences, decisions, recurring patterns. No buttons. No prompts.

step3.title: You curate (optional)
step3.body: Pin what matters. Forget what doesn't. Travel back in time to audit. The system runs without you, but it works better with you.

emptyOnboarding.title: Memory will appear here
emptyOnboarding.body: Every conversation your agents finish leaves a trace. Soon you'll review what they remembered, pin what matters, and forget what you don't want kept.
emptyOnboarding.step1Body: Anywhere — web widget, Slack, WhatsApp, email.
emptyOnboarding.step3Body: Curate, pin, or forget. Or just leave it running.

graph.title: Memory Graph
graph.emptyDesc: Start conversations — your agents will build this graph automatically.

charts.agentPerformanceSub: current month · ordered by token consumption

generic.title: Something went wrong

memory.title: Persistent memory

Review this flow and tell me, in plain language, whether it has errors, unconfigured steps, or missing connections. Do not use the set_flow tool, only explain:

{describe}

settings.deleteWarning: This action is irreversible. All data will be permanently deleted.
settings.teamDescription: Manage who has access to this workspace.

step1.title: Welcome to Orchester
step1.description: Let's set up your workspace in under 2 minutes.

step2.title: Connect your AI provider
step2.description: Add an API key to power your agents. You can change this later.

step3.title: Choose a starting point
step3.description: Pick a ready-made team or start from scratch.

step4.title: Load your employees
step4.description: Import your team so agents can greet them by name.
step4.skipForNow: Skip for now — I'll do this later

checklist.title: Get started
checklist.subtitle: 5 things to do first in your workspace

createAgent.title: Create your first agent
createAgent.description: Define a system prompt, model, and tools to give your team an AI teammate.

connectProvider.title: Connect a model provider
connectProvider.description: Add an API key so your agents can actually call a language model.

setupChannel.title: Set up a channel
setupChannel.description: Wire your agents to a web widget, WhatsApp, Slack, or email.

inviteTeammate.title: Invite a teammate
inviteTeammate.description: Bring someone in so the workspace isn't a one-person show.

firstRun.title: Run a flow or send a message
firstRun.description: Kick off your first conversation or flow run — proof it all works end-to-end.

delete.title: Delete {name}
delete.willHide: Hide the workspace from all members
delete.willEmail: Send you an email with a restore link
delete.windowExplainer: You have {days} days to restore. After that, all data is permanently deleted.
delete.confirmLabel: Type the workspace slug to confirm:

restore.title: Restore {name}
restore.tokenHint: Paste the token from the deletion email. Optional if you're the original owner.
restore.expiredOrInvalid: This recovery link is expired or invalid.
restore.expiredOrInvalidBody: If you believe this is a mistake, contact support — the workspace may already have been restored, the 30-day window may have passed, or you may need to be signed in as the owner.

suspended.title: This workspace is paused (read-only)
suspended.body: You can view data and export, but cannot edit or send messages.

transfer.title: Transfer ownership
transfer.intro: Transfer ownership of {name} to another admin. This is irreversible — you will become an admin.
transfer.noEligible: No other admins available. Promote a member to admin first.
transfer.passwordHint: We re-verify your password to defend against session hijack.
transfer.ackLabel: I understand I will become an admin and need to log in again
transfer.rateLimited: Too many attempts. Try again in {minutes} min.
transfer.notAMember: The selected user is no longer a member.

featureFlags.title: Feature flags
featureFlags.subtitle: Enable experimental features for this workspace.

listPage.title: Your workspaces
listPage.recentlyDeleted: Recently deleted workspaces can still be restored:

errors.notAMember: You're not a member of this workspace
errors.rateLimited: You're doing that too often. Try again in {minutes} min.
errors.slugInvalid: Use lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens (3-40 chars).
errors.slugTaken: That slug is already taken. Try {suggestion}.

memory.title: Memory operations
memory.subtitle: On-demand triggers for the Mnemosyne housekeeping crons. Each action enqueues a job that runs in the next worker tick.
memory.adminOnly: Only workspace admins can trigger memory operations.

health.name: Memory health snapshot
health.description: Recomputes the memory health snapshot for this workspace (fact counts, embedding coverage, recall hit-rate, contradictions).

dedup.name: Dedup pass
dedup.description: Merges near-duplicate facts (cosine ≥ 0.92) into a single primary record. Idempotent — re-runs find nothing to do.

prune.name: Prune inactive facts
prune.description: Archives unpinned facts with no recall hits, age > 90 days, and relevance < 0.1. Reversible from the archive.

consolidation.name: REM-style consolidation
consolidation.description: Clusters related facts and writes a one-sentence summary that supersedes them. Originals stay findable.

review-sweep.name: Review-queue sweep
review-sweep.description: Surfaces low-confidence facts into the review queue for human triage (cap 50 per run).

auto-pin.name: Auto-pin pass
auto-pin.description: Evaluates the auto-pin rule set against active facts and pins matches. Skips user-overridden rows.

summary.name: Summary refresh
summary.description: Pre-distills a per-(agent, user) memory summary so the next chat turn skips the LLM round-trip.
summary.requiresAgent: Trigger from agent settings — needs a specific agent.

confirm.title: Run {op}?
confirm.body: This will enqueue the job for this workspace. It runs on the next worker tick — usually within a minute.

toast.enqueued: Job enqueued — runs in the next worker tick.

common.autoConfirmedToast: Auto-confirmed — you skipped this prompt previously. {action} ran.

runtime.missingStepsWarning: Some steps could not load. The tour will skip them.
runtime.skippedStepNote: This step is not on the current page yet.

step1.title: Memory housekeeping, in one place
step1.body: This page collects every maintenance task your agents' long-term memory needs. Read each card, then run it on demand or trust the schedule. Nothing changes your data without a confirmation.

step2.title: Deduplicate similar facts
step2.body: Dedup merges facts that say the same thing in different words. It keeps recall sharp and reduces token cost on every query. Reversible from the audit log.

step3.title: Prune stale memory
step3.body: Prune archives facts that are old, unused, or contradict newer ones. Archived facts stay restorable for 30 days, so you decide on evidence, not on guesses.

step4.title: Run a task now
step4.body: Press Run now to start a task immediately. You will see an impact preview — scope, estimated cost, time, reversibility — before anything happens.

step1.title: Where conversations come in
step1.body: Channels are the inboxes your agents listen on — widget, Telegram, email and more. Connect one and the assigned agent starts answering real users from that surface.

step2.title: Pick a channel to connect
step2.body: Each card is a channel type. Pick one to open the form: name it, attach an agent, and paste credentials when the type needs them. Beta types are marked so expectations stay honest.

step3.title: Manage what is live
step3.body: Connected channels show their agent, status and webhook or embed snippet. Reassign the agent, rotate credentials or pause traffic without deleting the channel's history.

step4.title: Tune who answers
step4.body: Each channel is bound to one agent. The channel decides who can talk to it; the agent decides how to respond. Pick the agent in the channel's settings.

step1.title: Visual orchestration lives here
step1.body: Flows are the visual programs your agents run — branches, tools and human steps wired on a canvas. This page lists every flow in the workspace so you can audit what's live.

step2.title: Start a new flow
step2.body: Use this button to draft a flow from scratch or from a template. You pick the starting point now and design the canvas next; nothing runs until you save and connect a trigger.

step3.title: Each card is a live flow
step3.body: Node count and status show health at a glance. Click any card to open the canvas and edit, version or run it. Use this view to spot orphan flows before they reach production.

step4.title: Wire a flow to the world
step4.body: A flow only runs when something triggers it. Connect a channel to send real conversations through this orchestration, or add a knowledge base so the flow can cite sources.

step1.title: Mnemosyne control room
step1.body: This is the admin surface for Mnemosyne — the system that keeps long-term memory healthy. Every action here is workspace-scoped and admin-gated so you can audit it later.

step2.title: Housekeeping tasks
step2.body: Each card is one maintenance job — dedup, prune, consolidation. Run them on demand when you suspect drift, or trust the schedule. Destructive jobs always confirm first.

step3.title: Where to go next
step3.body: After running a task, jump to the Brain inspector to verify the impact on real facts, or open settings to tune which jobs run automatically.

step4.title: Recall quality controls
step4.body: Toggle the retrieval stages your agents use — HyDE, rerank, graph expansion — or pin a premium embedding model. Changes apply on the next query, not retroactively.

step1.title: Your memory, in plain view
step1.body: This is the Brain — every fact your agents have learned across conversations. The header lets you time-travel, export, or open the review queue when something looks off.

step2.title: Vital signs at a glance
step2.body: Counts, embedding coverage and trends so you can spot drift before it becomes noise. Check this dashboard first on Mondays, or after a heavy weekend of conversations.

step3.title: Find any fact, fast
step3.body: Filter by status, scope, source or recency. Combine filters to audit a noisy domain or to surface candidates worth pinning before they age out.

step4.title: Curate row by row
step4.body: Each row is one fact. Pin the keepers so agents always see them, forget the noise, or open citations to see exactly which message taught it.

step5.title: What to do next
step5.body: When a fact needs a second opinion, send it to the review queue. When you want a backup or audit, export the whole memory as JSON from these next steps.

step1.title: Your team of agents
step1.body: Agents are role-played workers in your workspace — each one has a persona, a model, and access to specific knowledge. This is the roster of every agent you've created.

step2.title: Filter by status
step2.body: Drafts are unfinished, active agents answer real conversations, inactive ones are paused. Filter here to focus on what needs attention before users notice.

step3.title: Each card is one agent
step3.body: Name, role, model and live status at a glance. Click a card to edit prompts, attach tools, or review who is currently chatting with it.

step4.title: Create a new agent
step4.body: Start from a template or from scratch. Templates seed the persona and prompt so you adjust details instead of starting from a blank screen.

step5.title: Connect agents to the world
step5.body: An agent without a channel or knowledge base is just a draft. Use these next steps to wire it to incoming messages and to the docs it should cite.

step1.title: The library your agents cite
step1.body: Knowledge bases are the docs, sites and files your agents read before answering. Group them by domain — product, policies, onboarding — so retrieval stays focused and citations stay accurate.

step2.title: Create a new base
step2.body: Start from a template or from scratch. Templates preset the embedding model and chunk size for common use cases so you avoid guessing at parameters you do not yet understand.

step3.title: Every card is one base
step3.body: Each card shows the embedding provider and model powering retrieval. Click a base to add sources, preview chunks, and test queries before pointing an agent at it.

step4.title: Next steps that matter
step4.body: A base with no documents answers nothing, and a base no agent reads helps nobody. Use these links to upload sources and to attach the base to the agent that needs it.

step1.title: Plug in the brains
step1.body: Providers are the LLM accounts your agents call — OpenAI, Anthropic, Google and more. Nothing runs until at least one is connected, because every reply depends on a model behind a key you control.

step2.title: Your live providers
step2.body: Each connected provider shows its key status and the models it unlocks. Rotate keys here when they expire or leak, and disconnect any provider whose models you no longer use.

step3.title: Browse the catalog
step3.body: Search or filter by capability — chat, embeddings, images, audio — to find a new provider. Connect one and its models become selectable across your agents and flows.

step4.title: Wire providers into work
step4.body: A connected provider only matters once something uses it. Open Agents to assign a model, or Knowledge to pick the embedding provider that powers retrieval.

memoryOps.pageSubtitle: This is where Mnemosyne keeps your agents' long-term memory tidy. Each card below is a maintenance task you can run on demand or leave on its schedule. Read what each task does before running it; some change your data and we want you to decide on evidence.
memoryOps.adminOnly: Only workspace owners and admins can run housekeeping tasks. You can still review what each one does.
memoryOps.confirmDialogDescription: Review the impact below before confirming. Nothing happens until you press the action button.
memoryOps.reversibilityArchive: Archived for 30 days, restorable from the archive view.
memoryOps.reversibilityDestructive: Destructive. Archived facts are permanently removed and cannot be recovered.
memoryOps.noEstimateAvailable: We don't have a precise estimate for this task. It usually completes within a minute.
memoryOps.successToastBody: We'll notify you here when it finishes. {time, select, unknown {} other {Estimated time: {time}.}}
memoryOps.errorToastBody: {message}. Check your connection and try again. If the problem continues, review the system status page.

openBrain.title: Open the Brain inspector
openBrain.body: Review individual facts, see when they were learned, and decide which ones to keep.

reviewPolicy.title: Review your memory policy
reviewPolicy.body: Adjust which tasks run automatically and how often.

summaryAgentCallout.title: This task runs per agent
summaryAgentCallout.body: Memory summaries are scoped to a single agent. Open the agent's settings and run the refresh from its memory tab.

healthSnapshot.name: Take a health snapshot
healthSnapshot.description: Measures the current size, growth rate, and duplication levels of your memory. Run it before any other task so you can compare before and after.
healthSnapshot.whyMatters: A snapshot is the baseline that lets every other change be measured.

dedup.name: Merge duplicate facts
dedup.description: Scans every fact, finds pairs that mean almost the same thing, and combines them into a single cleaner record. Use it when your agents start repeating themselves.
dedup.whyMatters: Fewer duplicates means faster recall and less repetition in answers.

prune.name: Remove unused facts
prune.description: Archives facts that have not been recalled in a long time and that no active agent depends on. The archive is reversible for 30 days.
prune.whyMatters: Smaller memory is cheaper to search and easier to keep accurate.

remConsolidation.name: Consolidate related facts
remConsolidation.description: Groups facts that belong to the same topic and merges them into a single summary. Inspired by how sleep consolidates memories in the human brain.
remConsolidation.whyMatters: Consolidated facts give shorter, more focused answers when an agent is asked a broad question.

reviewSweep.name: Queue facts for human review
reviewSweep.description: Finds facts with low confidence, conflicting sources, or unusual sources and adds them to your review queue. Nothing is deleted; you decide what to do with each item.
reviewSweep.whyMatters: Human review catches the cases the system is not sure about, before they affect an answer.

autoPin.name: Pin frequently recalled facts
autoPin.description: Identifies the facts your agents rely on the most and pins them so they always rank first in recall. You can unpin any of them at any time.
autoPin.whyMatters: Pinning the right facts keeps your most important answers consistent across conversations.

summaryRefresh.name: Refresh memory summaries
summaryRefresh.description: Regenerates the short summaries that appear next to each topic in Brain. Run it after a large import or after a consolidation pass.
summaryRefresh.whyMatters: Up-to-date summaries make it easier for you to scan what your agents know without opening every fact.

recall.title: Recall quality
recall.subtitle: Three recall enhancements run by default on every turn. Each adds a small cost. Turn one off only if you have measured that the savings matter more to you than the quality.
recall.tip: Changes apply to the next recall turn. Existing answers are not regenerated.
recall.savedToast: Recall settings saved. The next conversation turn uses them.
recall.saveErrorToast: We couldn't save your recall settings. {message}. Check your connection and try again. If the problem continues, see the system status page.

hyde.label: HyDE — hypothetical document embedding
hyde.help: Generates a short fake answer to the user's question and uses that for the search instead of the raw question. This closes the gap between how questions and answers look in embedding space. Adds one cheap model call per turn.

rerank.label: Cross-encoder rerank
rerank.help: Reorders the top results using a second pass that scores each result against the question together. Uses Cohere when its API key is set; otherwise a local fallback. Adds about 50 milliseconds per turn.

graph.label: Graph expansion
graph.help: After the initial recall, follows links between facts (derived from, supersedes, part of) to surface related ones. One extra database query. Improves recall quality by about 10 to 15 percent.

premium.title: Premium embedding model
premium.body: Use a richer embedding model for the facts that matter most: pinned, high confidence, or workspace-wide. Other facts continue to use the default model so cost stays predictable. Leave the provider empty to disable this tier.

schedule.modalSubtitle: Choose when this task should run for this workspace.

modes.hourlyHint: At most every hour (subject to the global cron).
modes.dailyHint: At most every 24h (subject to the global cron).
modes.customHint: A cron expression (5 fields). Gate treats it as daily.

schedule.customHelp: Standard 5-field format: minute hour day-of-month month day-of-week.
schedule.maxFreqNote: Your choice acts as a maximum: it cannot run more often than the global cron.
schedule.disabledWarning: This task will not run for this workspace. Turn it on again whenever you want.

brain.title: No memories yet
brain.body: Brain shows everything your agents have learned from past conversations. Once an agent handles its first conversation, the facts it learns appear here for you to review and curate.

flows.title: No flows yet
flows.body: A flow is a sequence of agents and steps that runs together to complete a task. Create your first flow to chain agents and let them hand work to each other on demand or on a schedule.

channels.title: No channels connected
channels.body: A channel is the doorway between an agent and your users, such as a website widget or a messaging app. Connect one to start receiving real conversations.

providers.title: No providers connected
providers.body: A provider is the company that supplies the AI model your agents use, like OpenAI or Anthropic. Connect at least one with your own API key so your agents can start answering.

knowledgeBases.title: No knowledge bases yet
knowledgeBases.body: Knowledge bases give your agents the documents they should rely on when answering. Upload your first file or connect a source so agents can quote your own information instead of guessing.

agents.title: No agents yet
agents.body: An agent is a configured assistant with its own prompt, model, and tools. Create your first agent and give it a clear role so you can start routing conversations to it.

mnemosyneAdmin.title: Nothing scheduled yet
mnemosyneAdmin.body: This is where you control the maintenance tasks that keep memory healthy. Enable the tasks you want to run on a schedule, or trigger any of them manually when you need a one-off pass.

brain.pageSubtitlePart1: Every durable fact your agents have learned, with the filters you need to audit and curate it. Memory is powered by
brain.pageSubtitlePart2: , the layer that keeps long-term context consistent across conversations.

tip.title: Read before you forget
tip.bodyPart1: Forgetting a fact removes it from recall, but the original record stays in the audit log.
tip.bodyPart2: is reversible from the Undo view, so curate freely.

brain.embeddedHelp: Share of facts that already have an
brain.embeddedHelpSuffix: and can be found by semantic search.

forget.title: Forget this fact?
forget.description: The fact stops appearing in recall results. Agents will not use it on their next turn. The original record stays in the audit log.

review.title: Work the review queue
review.body: Curate low-confidence and conflicting facts before they shape an answer.

housekeeping.title: Run memory housekeeping
housekeeping.body: Merge duplicates, prune unused facts, and consolidate related ones.

flows.heroSubtitlePart3: and tools into a single sequence so a job runs end-to-end. Use this page to see every flow in this workspace, create new ones, and open them in the visual editor.
flows.createHelp: Pick a short, descriptive name. You can rename it later from the editor.
flows.firstFlowTip: Start small: one trigger and two or three steps is enough to see a flow run end-to-end. You can expand it once the basics work.

nextStepConnectChannel.title: Connect a channel
nextStepConnectChannel.body: Hook a website widget or messaging app to a flow so real conversations can trigger it.

nextStepAddKnowledge.title: Add a knowledge base
nextStepAddKnowledge.body: Give your agents documents to ground their answers in, instead of relying on the base model alone.

knowledgeBases.heroSubtitlePart1: A knowledge base is a collection of documents your agents can quote when they answer. We turn each document into
knowledgeBases.heroSubtitlePart2: so agents recall by meaning, not just exact words — the technique commonly called
knowledgeBases.heroSubtitlePart3: . Use this page to see every base in this workspace and create new ones.
knowledgeBases.createHelp: Pick a short, descriptive name. You can rename it later from the base's settings.
knowledgeBases.embeddingHelp: The embedding model decides how your documents become searchable. Keep the default unless you have a reason to switch.
knowledgeBases.createError: We could not create the knowledge base. Check your connection and try again.
knowledgeBases.firstBaseTipBody: Start with one source — a single PDF or a short folder of docs is enough to see how recall behaves. You can add more sources once the first one works.

nextStepUploadDocs.title: Upload your first documents
nextStepUploadDocs.body: Open a base and add PDFs, text or URLs so its agents can quote them in answers.

nextStepConnectAgent.title: Attach a base to an agent
nextStepConnectAgent.body: Pick an agent and connect this base so its replies are grounded in your own information.

agents.heroSubtitlePart2: is a configured assistant with its own
agents.heroSubtitlePart3: , model, and tools. Use this page to see every agent in the workspace, change their role, and decide which conversations each one should handle.
agents.modelTipTitle: Pick the right model for the job
agents.modelTipBody: Use Haiku for short, high-volume replies and Sonnet or Opus for longer reasoning or multi-step work. You can change the model on any agent without losing its history.
agents.deleteImpactDescription: Deleting an agent removes its configuration. Past conversations stay in the workspace but stop receiving new replies from this agent.
agents.deleteImpactReversibilityValue: Permanent. The agent and its prompt cannot be restored.

nextStepConnectChannel.title: Connect a channel
nextStepConnectChannel.body: Hook a website widget or messaging app to an agent so real conversations can reach it.

nextStepAddKnowledge.title: Add knowledge
nextStepAddKnowledge.body: Upload documents so your agents quote your own information instead of relying on the base model alone.

channels.heroSubtitlePart3: and your users — a website widget, a Telegram bot, an HTTP endpoint. Connect one and your agent starts answering real conversations through it.
channels.tipBody: Each channel routes incoming messages to a single agent. You can pause, reassign, or disconnect a channel at any time without losing past conversations.
channels.deleteDescription: Disconnecting stops new messages from arriving on this channel. Past conversations stay in your workspace; you can reconnect later, but the public URLs and secrets will be regenerated.
channels.deleteImpactReversibilityValue: Reconnecting later regenerates the public URL and the secret token.

nextStepConfigureAgent.title: Configure the answering agent
nextStepConfigureAgent.body: Give the agent the right prompt, tools, and knowledge so it answers correctly when a message arrives through this channel.

nextStepReviewConversations.title: Review live conversations
nextStepReviewConversations.body: Open the conversations view to see what users are asking and how your agents respond.

providers.heroSubtitle: Connect the companies that power your agents. Each provider exposes a set of models for chat, embeddings, images, audio and more. Your keys stay encrypted and scoped to this workspace.
providers.tipBody: Orchester never marks up provider costs. Connect your own API key and every model billing flows directly through your account with that provider.
providers.removeConfirmDescription: Your stored API key is deleted from this workspace. Agents and flows that rely on this provider will stop responding until you connect another key.
providers.removeImpactReversibilityValue: Reconnect anytime by pasting a new key.
providers.removeError: We couldn't disconnect {provider}. Check your connection and try again. If the problem continues, see the system status page.
providers.removeSuccess: {provider} disconnected. Agents that relied on it won't respond until you connect another provider.

nextStepBuildAgent.title: Assign a model to an agent
nextStepBuildAgent.body: Pick a model from a connected provider and wire it into the agent that should use it.

nextStepAddKnowledge.title: Add knowledge to recall
nextStepAddKnowledge.body: Upload documents so your agents can ground answers in your data instead of guessing.

subtitle.agent: Pick a starting point. You can edit the role, prompt, and model before saving.
subtitle.flow: Pick a starting point. The canvas opens pre-wired so you can adapt instead of starting blank.
subtitle.knowledge: Pick a starting point. Embedding settings can be tuned later from the base settings.
subtitle.channel: Pick a channel type. You'll add credentials and assign an agent in the next step.

shell.footerHint: Templates are starting points — you can change anything afterwards.

blank.label: Blank agent
blank.description: Start from scratch. Empty role, empty prompt, your call.

support-tier1.label: Support Tier 1
support-tier1.description: Answers customer questions grounded in your knowledge bases. Escalates when confidence is low.

lead-qualifier.label: Lead Qualifier
lead-qualifier.description: Qualifies inbound leads with BANT scoring and returns a structured summary.

sales-coach.label: Sales Coach
sales-coach.description: Reviews sales calls and gives account executives three specific things to try next time.

internal-helpdesk.label: Internal Helpdesk
internal-helpdesk.description: Answers IT and HR questions for employees. Opens a ticket when a human is needed.

onboarding-guide.label: Onboarding Guide
onboarding-guide.description: Walks new users through the shortest path to their first win in the product.

blank.label: Blank flow
blank.description: Empty canvas with a single Trigger node so you can wire your own logic.

lead-qualification.label: Lead Qualification
lead-qualification.description: Webhook intake, enrichment, qualifier agent, branch on score.

support-triage.label: Support Triage
support-triage.description: Inbound message, KB search, agent answers, escalates if confidence is low.

newsletter-compile.label: Newsletter Compile
newsletter-compile.description: Pulls updates on a schedule, agent writes the issue, sends it.

pr-review-bot.label: PR Review Bot
pr-review-bot.description: GitHub webhook, fetch diff, review agent, post a comment back on the PR.

blank.label: Blank knowledge base
blank.description: Empty base. Add documents after — we'll chunk and embed in the background.

product-docs.label: Product Docs
product-docs.description: Public documentation, changelogs, and feature guides for your product.

hr-policies.label: HR Policies
hr-policies.description: Vacation, benefits, code of conduct, and other employee-facing policies.

engineering-wiki.label: Engineering Wiki
engineering-wiki.description: Architecture notes, runbooks, on-call docs, and ADRs.

brand-voice.label: Brand Voice
brand-voice.description: Tone of voice, banned words, on-brand and off-brand examples.

web-widget.label: Web widget
web-widget.description: Embed a chat widget on your site. Comes with a default greeting you can edit.

whatsapp.label: WhatsApp
whatsapp.description: Connect a WhatsApp Business number so customers can chat from their phone.

slack.label: Slack
slack.description: Bring an agent into your Slack workspace. Great for internal helpdesks.

email.label: Email
email.description: Forward an inbox to an agent. Replies arrive in the same thread.

api.label: API
api.description: Talk to an agent over HTTP. For custom integrations and your own apps.

common.sessionExpiredBody: Sign in again to continue setting up your first agent. Your progress is saved.

welcome.heading: Let's get your first agent live.
welcome.subhead: Five short steps. By the end you'll have a working agent answering messages — and you can keep building from there.
welcome.question: What are you here to build?

customerSupport.label: Answer customers with AI
customerSupport.hint: Tier-1 support, lead capture, FAQ deflection.

internalAutomation.label: Automate internal tasks
internalAutomation.hint: Helpdesk, ops bots, knowledge lookup for employees.

exploring.label: I'm exploring
exploring.hint: I want to see what this can do before committing.

sample.label: Work with sample data (Acme Inc.)
sample.hint: Drops you into a pre-seeded workspace with agents, conversations, and a knowledge base ready to inspect.
sample.unavailable: Available once your account has the Acme Inc. seed workspace.

provider.heading: Connect an AI provider.
provider.subhead: Add a key to power your agents. We'll handle the rest — your key never leaves this server.
provider.security: Stored encrypted at rest. Used only to call the provider on your behalf.
provider.errorFallback: We could not connect the provider. Check the key and try again.
provider.errorNetwork: Network error reaching the server. Check your connection and try again.

agent.heading: Create your first agent.
agent.subhead: An agent has a name, a role, and a model. Pick a template to skip the blank page — you can rewrite anything later.

helpdesk.label: Internal helpdesk
helpdesk.hint: IT and HR questions for employees.

salesCoach.label: Sales coach
salesCoach.hint: Reviews calls and proposes next steps.

agent.rolePlaceholder: One line: what does this agent do?
agent.errorFallback: The agent could not be created. Review the form and try again.
agent.errorNetwork: Network error reaching the server. Check your connection and try again.

talk.heading: Talk to {name}.
talk.subhead: Send a message to confirm your agent is wired up. One message and one reply is enough to continue.
talk.emptyHint: Send the first message to start the conversation.
talk.errorFallback: The agent did not reply. Check the provider key and try again.
talk.errorEmpty: The provider returned an empty response. Try a different prompt.
talk.errorNetwork: Network error reaching the server. Check your connection and try again.

done.heading: Your agent answered its first message.
done.subhead: You're ready to build the rest of the stack. Three short follow-ups will compound the result.

channel.title: Connect a channel
channel.body: Wire the agent to WhatsApp, web chat, or email so real users can reach it.

knowledge.title: Add knowledge
knowledge.body: Ground answers in your docs. Upload a PDF or point at a URL.

brain.title: Inspect Brain memory
brain.body: See what your agent has learned. Memory is what makes a second conversation feel personal.

help.title: How can we help?
help.commandBarHint: Tip: press ⌘K / Ctrl-K to search anything
help.askCommunityBody: Orchester is open source. Reach maintainers and other operators on GitHub Discussions. We usually respond within a day.

default.title: Need help?
default.body: Search any term above, restart the page tour, or jump back into onboarding. Everything you set up here is reversible.

brain.title: About Brain memory
brain.body: Brain is what your agents have learned across conversations. Pin the facts that matter, forget the noise, and audit changes before they reach production.

memory.title: Memory housekeeping
memory.body: Open the memory housekeeping page to inspect or rebuild the memory layer. You will see scope and reversibility before each action.

flows.title: About Flows
flows.body: Flows are the visual programs your agents run end to end. Branches, tools, and human handoffs live here so logic stays inspectable.

channels.title: About Channels
channels.body: Channels are the inboxes your agents listen on: widget, Slack, WhatsApp, email. Connect one to send real conversations into a flow.

providers.title: About Providers
providers.body: Providers are the LLM accounts your agents call. Connect at least one before agents or flows can run.

knowledge.title: About Knowledge Bases
knowledge.body: Knowledge Bases ground answers in your docs. Smaller bases focused on one domain retrieve more accurately than one giant pile.

agents.title: About Agents
agents.body: Agents are the role-played workers in your org: persona, tools, memory. Every prompt change becomes a version you can roll back.

whatsNew.empty: Release notes will land here when the next version ships.

helpDrawer.title: Help drawer arrives
helpDrawer.body: Press ? anywhere to search articles, restart a tour, or jump back into onboarding.

brainHealth.title: Brain health dashboard
brainHealth.body: Embedding coverage and dedup trends now show at the top of Brain so drift is easy to catch.

flowTemplates.title: Flow templates
flowTemplates.body: Start from a template so you can adapt prewired logic instead of building flow graphs from scratch.
